mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, and PDGFR signaling to block AML cell proliferation, particularly FLT3‑ITD–positive cells
additional_notes: Potent against FLT3‑ITD AML; strong relevance to MOLM‑13 (FLT3‑ITD+)

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and multiple other kinases to suppress proliferation and survival signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; strong activity reported in MOLM‑13

mechanism: Highly selective FLT3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor, blocking FLT3‑ITD signaling and inducing apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; highly potent against FLT3‑ITD AML cell lines including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL kinases, disrupting survival signaling in FLT3‑mutated AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutated AML; strong activity in MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits Src family kinases and BCR‑ABL, interfering with signaling nodes such as SRC and LYN used by AML cells
additional_notes: Broad tyrosine kinase inhibitor; some activity in FLT3‑mutant AML contexts

mechanism: Targets FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, and PDGFR to inhibit proliferative and survival signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Reported activity in AML, including FLT3‑mutant lines

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, c‑KIT, and PDGFR, reducing oncogenic kinase signaling relevant in some AML subsets
additional_notes: Documented anti‑proliferative effects in AML cell lines; more potent analog of imatinib

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, PDGFR, and c‑KIT, blocking growth signaling in kinase‑driven leukemias and some AML subsets
additional_notes: Baseline TKI for comparison; repurposing mainly in c‑KIT or PDGFR‑driven AML; non‑BCR‑ABL AML cells typically show low‑µM IC50s

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; standard agent in AML combinations; strong activity in many AML lines including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits anti‑apoptotic proteins BCL‑2 and BCL‑xL, triggering apoptosis in hematologic malignancies
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; strong preclinical AML data; can sensitize MOLM‑13 to various agents

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, reducing JAK/STAT pathway signaling involved in AML proliferation and survival
additional_notes: Relevant for AML subsets with activated JAK/STAT and for FLT3‑mutant contexts via downstream signaling

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, dampening cytokine‑driven JAK/STAT signaling that can support AML cell survival
additional_notes: Approved for rheumatoid arthritis; repurposing candidate for AML microenvironment‑mediated signaling

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, suppressing protein synthesis and growth signaling downstream of PI3K/AKT in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling is hyperactive in AML including MOLM‑13; synergy reported with FLT3 or BCL‑2 inhibitors

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling, leading to decreased cell growth and proliferation in AML models
additional_notes: Similar rationale to everolimus; active in AML models and used in combination with tyrosine kinase inhibitors

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, altering chromatin structure and gene expression to induce differentiation and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; epigenetic therapy with extensive AML cell line data; synergizes with FLT3 and BCL‑2 inhibitors

mechanism: Broadly inhibits class I/II HDACs, leading to hyperacetylation of histones and non‑histone proteins and triggering apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; very potent epigenetic agent; reported to inhibit proliferation and induce apoptosis in FLT3‑ITD AML lines

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNMTs, causing DNA hypomethylation and reactivation of silenced genes in AML cells
additional_notes: Hypomethylating agent (HMA); already used clinically in AML; extended exposure (48–96 h) recommended for cell assays

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA and DNA and inhibits DNMTs, leading to hypomethylation and epigenetic reprogramming in AML cells
additional_notes: HMA standard in AML; effective range ~0.5–10 µM with prolonged exposure (72–96 h); used as epigenetic modulator in MOLM‑13 and other lines

mechanism: Activates AMPK and indirectly inhibits mTOR, altering cellular energy metabolism and reducing growth of AML cells at high concentrations
additional_notes: Broad anticancer repurposing candidate; AML cells can be sensitive in high‑µM to low‑mM range; monitor osmolarity and medium pH in assays

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, particularly with copper, induces oxidative and proteotoxic stress leading to AML cell death
additional_notes: Often tested with copper supplementation (e.g., CuCl₂ or CuSO₄ at 0.5–5 µM constant); targets ALDH‑positive leukemic stem/progenitor cells; AML line data show µM‑range cytotoxicity
